steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that bees “forage beyond patches of low species richness to acquire multiple resource types.” This directly reveals why bees ignore the density model: dense patches must lack variety in flower types.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Homogeneous flowers (all the same type) explain why bees abandon a dense patch — even though flowers are abundant, they're all identical, forcing bees to travel beyond for different resource types. Depleted, immature, and dispersed either miss the species richness issue or contradict the dense patch premise itself. That matches choice B — B is correct.
